sentences: At sixty-four years old, Grace stands on the beach in Florida.
She tried to swim this distance when she was twenty-eight.
She failed then and did not swim for thirty years.
But now, at sixty-four, she tries again and hopes for success.
Grace was a champion swimmer when she was young.
She swam around Manhattan Island last summer.
She swam between the Bahamas and Florida.
She set records and became famous around the world.
The swim in Cuba was different and exciting.
It was not about sports and not about records.
It was a dream that did not let her go.
Grace had a poster in her room when she was a child.
A diamond is coal that stays strong, and she values this.
She always believes in not giving up.
Grace failed the Cuba swim at twenty-eight and gave up swimming.
Grace worked as a sports journalist for thirty years.
She goes to the Olympic Games.
She covers the Tour de France and shares the news.
She wrote about the best athletes and their great skills.
It was a good life, but something felt wrong.
When she turned sixty, she saw she was not a doer.
She only watches other people do things.
She feels bad and is bothered by this.
Grace wants to swim again.
She chased her dream again but everyone said it was impossible.
She is sixty years old now.
Her body is older now than before.
Her muscles are weak now.
But her dream is still alive.
The first attempt did not work, but we try again.
The second try did not work.
The third try does not work.
The fourth try does not work.
Each failure is painful and makes us feel sad.
Each failure made people say Grace should give up.
Grace remembers her poster and feels happy about it.
A diamond is a piece of coal that stayed strong.
She sticks with it and does not give up.
Grace is sixty-four years old on her fifth try.
She swam for fifty-three hours without stopping.
She swam one hundred miles in dangerous water and felt strong.
Sharks swam close to us in the water.
A jellyfish stung her when she swam in the sea.
The sun burned her skin and made her feel hot.
But she continued to swim.
When Grace reached the beach in Florida, many people waited.
They cry because they feel sad.
They feel strong emotions and show their feelings.
They were not fans of sports and did not watch games.
These people watched Grace and her message to never give up.
Grace is afraid of wasting her one precious life.
She did not let her life go by.
She wants to have a bold and exciting adventure.
She did it and felt happy.
At sixty-four, she shows that dreams do not end.
Age is only a number and does not matter.
Grace's story is not only about swimming but also about life.
It was also about learning new things and having fun.
Grace wanted to finish her education when she was younger.
She started college, but life made it hard for her.
She leaves school to help her family and be with them.
She had a good job but missed finishing her degree.
Grace thought it was too late when she got older.
She put her dreams of education behind her.
She worked hard and forgot her dreams.
After the Cuba swim, Grace realized something important.
It is not too late to start.
Grace wants to go back to school now.
At sixty-five, she starts college classes and learns new things.
She sat in class with students younger than her children.
Some of them looked at her in a strange way.
Some of them asked why Grace was there.
Grace did not care about what they think.
She is there to learn new things.
The classes are difficult and many students feel tired.
Grace's mind is not as sharp as when she was twenty.
She must study harder for her tests and homework.
She worked longer hours because she needed more money.
But she did not stop trying.
She remembers her swimming journey and feels happy.
She remembered that trying hard was what mattered most.
Grace studied for four years and graduated from college.
She is sixty-nine years old now.
Her family gave her a surprise party for graduation.
They invited all of Grace's friends to the party.
They gave her a cap and a gown.
When Grace wore the cap and gown, she felt very emotional.
Grace's diploma is on her dresser and looks very nice.
She looks at it every morning and feels happy.
It reminds her that it is not too late.
It reminds her that dreams never end and can come true.
It shows her that age is only a number.
Grace's change is about swimming and learning new things.
It was also about Grace's health and how she feels.
When Grace was fifty-six, she worked as a secretary.
She never exercises and she did not exercise before.
She was prissy and did not want to mess her hair.
She does not want to break her nails.
Exercise was not a part of her life before.
One day, Grace went shopping with her sister Rose.
They look for swimsuits for a pool party.
They tried on swimsuits and laughed at each other in the mirror.
They do not look good and feel bad.
They looked older, but they felt young inside.
Rose told Grace they must do something and they made a promise.
They start to exercise.
They want to be healthy and feel good.
They become strong people, no matter how old they are.
Grace and Rose work out together.
They lifted weights and felt strong.
They ran fast and felt happy.
They changed their diets and ate different foods.
It was hard at first, but it got easier later.
Grace's body hurts and she feels tired.
Her muscles are sore after a long workout.
But she continued to move forward.
She and Rose help each other and feel good.
They did this together.
A year later, Rose died suddenly and everyone was sad.
She had a problem in her brain.
Grace felt very sad and was devastated.
She fell into a deep sadness and felt very alone.
She cannot do anything now or later.
She cannot work out and feels bad.
She cannot eat because she feels sick.
She can only think about Rose now.
But Grace remembered the promise she made with Rose.
Rose wanted them to be strong.
Rose wanted them to show that age does not matter.
Grace decides to continue with her plans.
She keeps the promise and feels happy.
She honors Rose's memory and remembers her with love.
Grace feels strong and gets herself together.
She found a trainer and felt happy about it.
She works out three days a week.
She worked out five days a week and felt strong.
She worked out every day and felt strong.
Her body changes and looks different now.
Her health is better now.
She had high blood pressure and panic attacks and acid reflux.
When she started exercising, all of that went away.
Grace is a bodybuilder and she competes.
She enters competitions and tries to win.
She won her first competition and felt very happy.
She is the oldest female bodybuilder in the world.
She lifts one hundred and fifty pounds on the bench.
She runs and runs eighty miles each week.
She feels better now than she felt at forty.
People ask Grace how she does it.
They said she is too old.
They said it is too late now.
Grace has a message for them.
She said exercise is the best way to be healthy and it is never too late.
It is never too late to change your life, as Grace shows.
Dreams do not end and they can still come true.
Age is just a number and it does not matter.
It shows us that it is never too late to begin again.
Perseverance is more important than anything else in life.
Grace shows you can reach your dreams at any age.
She showed that you can learn when you are older.
She showed that you can change your health at any age.
She shows that you should not give up, no matter your age.
When Grace was sixty-four, she swam again and won.
When she was sixty-nine, she finished college and graduated.
When she was fifty-six, she started to exercise and won.
She did this because she did not believe it was too late.
Grace's message is simple: always try and never give up.
Believe in your dream and do not give up to succeed.
You can reach your other shore when you try.
Age does not matter and everyone can be happy.
Time does not matter for Grace and Rose.
What is important is that you keep going.
Grace is in her seventies now.
She swims for health and fitness every day.
She exercises every day and feels strong.
She learns new things every day and enjoys it.
She still chases her dreams and wants to be happy.
She shows that life can be good after sixty.
Life continues after seventy and can be good.
Life goes on when you want to live it fully.
Grace's story inspires many people in the world.
People write to her and she writes back.
They say she changed their lives for the better.
They tell her it is never too late to change.
They say she gives them hope and they feel happy.
Grace has a message that is for everyone.
It is for people who think they are too old for school.
It is for people who think they are too old to exercise.
It is for the person who thinks their dream can happen.
Grace says it is never too late for anyone who thinks so.
Always try hard and do not stop.
Your dreams wait for you to find them.
Your other shore is waiting for you and it is near.
You just need to begin swimming to start.
